title: senecio pseuderucoides
intro: Senecio pseuderucoides, also known as Groundsel Bush, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand. It contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Groundsel Bush can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
